perturbation.original_text: 5. Remedies: If either Party causes a disclosure of the other Party’s Confidential Information in breach of the terms of this Agreement, the disclosing Party shall immediately report in writing the disclosure to the other Party and shall save, defend, indemnify and hold the non-disclosing Party harmless from and against any and all liability and damages suffered by the non-disclosing Party arising therefrom. In addition to the foregoing and without limitation thereof, the disclosing Party shall cooperate in prosecuting any claims against third parties for unauthorized use of any Confidential Information. Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure, use or disposition, whether actual or threatened, of any Confidential Information shall cause irreparable harm, loss of business and significant injury to the disclosing Party, the scope of which would be difficult to ascertain. Each Party agrees, therefore, that the disclosing Party has the right to obtain an immediate injunction against any breach, threatened breach or attempted breach of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedies that may be available at law, including without limitation, the recovery of expenses, costs and attorney’s fees arising out of such breach, threatened breach or attempted breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Remedies: If either Party causes a disclosure of the other Party’s Confidential Information in breach of the terms of this Agreement, the disclosing Party may, at its sole discretion, report in writing the disclosure to the other Party. The disclosing party is not obligated to defend, indemnify, or hold harmless the non-disclosing party. Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure, use or disposition of any Confidential Information may or may not cause harm to the disclosing Party. Each Party agrees that the disclosing Party may, but is not required to, seek legal remedies for any breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates immediate reporting, indemnification, and acknowledges irreparable harm from disclosure. The modified version makes reporting optional, removes the indemnification obligation, questions the harm caused by disclosure, and makes seeking legal remedies discretionary. This creates significant uncertainty about the available remedies, contradicting the original strong protections for Confidential Information. By removing the *shall* to *may* it turns the obligation into something that is not legally enforcable
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Definition of "Confidential Information": The term "Confidential Information" shall mean all information disclosed by one Party to the other Party, whether orally, in written, electronic or other format, and whether disclosed by a Party’s agents, principals, employees or representatives, and whether to the other Party's agent's principals, employees or representatives. “Confidential Information” shall include, without limitation, all ideas improvements, inventions, methodologies, works and other innovations of any kind, authored, conceived, developed, made or reduced to practice by the disclosing Party, whether or not eligible for copyright, patent, trademark, trade secret or other legal protection (including, without limitation, formulas, processes, databases, mechanical and electronic hardware, electronic components, computers and their parts, computer programs and their documentation, encoding techniques, marketing and new product plans, production, processes, advertising, packaging and marketing techniques, marketing plans, product plans, technical plans, business strategies, strategic alliances and partners, financial information, engineering data, methodologies and processes, forecasts, personnel information, customer and prospective customer lists, trade secrets, product design, capabilities, specifications, the identify of potential and actual customers, and suppliers and all documentation, materials and media provided by one Party to the other).
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the protections for 'Confidential Information' as defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The original text mandates actions and acknowledges harm, while the changed text makes these discretionary and questions the harm. This creates a direct conflict because the definition of Confidential Information assumes strong protection, which is undermined by the discretionary remedies in the changed text. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the altered clause contradicts the understanding and enforceability implied in the definition of 'Confidential Information'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Return of "Confidential Information": All Confidential Information shall be returned to the disclosing Parties promptly upon written request or, at the election of the disclosing Party, the Party that received the Confidential Information shall certify said information has been destroyed and is no longer useable in any format.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return of "Confidential Information": Confidential Information need not be returned unless specifically requested and the receiving party agrees to do so. The method of return, or destruction, if applicable, will be determined by the receiving party at their sole discretion. There is no requirement to certify destruction.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause creates a clear obligation to return or destroy Confidential Information. The modified version removes this obligation by stating that the Confidential Information need not be returned unless the receiving party agrees and that the method of returning/destroying the information is up to receiving party, meaning the information might not be destroyed and can cause harm to the disclosure party. This contradicts the foundational purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Term, Assignment and Survival: This Agreement shall be valid unless terminated by mutual written Agreement. Each Party’s obligations with respect to the Confidential Information, including but not limited to, sections 2,4,5,6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and/or return of all Confidential Information from the latter date of either termination or return of such information. Neither this Agreement nor any rights hereunder, in whole or in part, shall be assignable or otherwise transferable by either Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of Confidential Information, whereas the changed text makes return conditional on the receiving party's agreement and grants them sole discretion over the method, potentially negating destruction. The contradicted text (Section 9) states that obligations regarding Confidential Information, specifically including Section 6, survive termination. The changed Section 6 contradicts this survival clause because if the receiving party does not agree to return/destroy as per the changed Section 6, then the survival of obligation to return/destroy is nullified, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Prohibition of Disclosure: Neither Party hereto shall in any way or in any form distribute, disclose, publicize, issue press releases, or advertise in any manner, including, but not limited to, making representation in court pleadings, except as required by law, the discussions that gave rise to this Agreement, the discussions or negotiations covered by this Agreement, this Agreement or the Confidential Information provided pursuant to this Agreement, without first obtaining the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Prohibition of Disclosure: Either Party hereto may disclose or publicize the Confidential Information, discussions that gave rise to this Agreement, the discussions or negotiations covered by this Agreement, this Agreement without first obtaining the prior written consent of the other Party. The original text said that neither parties can disclose without consent, now both parties can disclose
perturbation.explanation: The original clause has a restriction on disclosure of Confidential Information. The changed version explicitly states that a party *can* disclose Confidential Information, the discussion that lead up to the agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party. This completely voids the purpose of the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Protection of "Confidential Information": In consideration of each Party's disclosure of Confidential Information to the other Party, each Party agrees with respect to the Confidential Information received from the other Party, that it: (a) shall maintain such Confidential Information in the strictest confidence; Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096 www.Pareteum.com NYSE MKT: TEUM (b) shall not disclose, transfer or otherwise make available any of such Confidential Information to any third party, unless such Confidential Information must be disclosed for the purposes contemplated herein, or under legal compulsion to disclose any such Confidential Information, in which event each Party shall, prior to such disclosure, obtain written consent from the other Party and obtain from the third person a written agreement acknowledging the binding effect of these restrictions regarding the Confidential Information; and (c) shall not directly, indirectly or in concert with any person, use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than evaluating the prospective business relationship with the other Party in accordance with the introduction . Each Party shall take reasonable measures to protect the Confidential Information of the other Party. Those measures shall not be less than the measures taken to protect the receiving Party’s own confidential information. Confidential Information of the other Party may be provided to a Party’s employees only on a need-to-know basis, and prior to such provision, the Party will notify each employee to whom such disclosure is made that such Confidential Information is received in confidence and shall be kept in confidence by such employee.
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 8 prohibited disclosure of confidential information without prior written consent. The changed text in clause 8 now allows either party to disclose this information without consent. This directly contradicts clause 2, which explicitly states that each party must maintain confidential information in the strictest confidence and not disclose it to any third party without written consent. The change creates a direct conflict in the obligations regarding confidential information, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Term, Assignment and Survival: This Agreement shall be valid unless terminated by mutual written Agreement. Each Party’s obligations with respect to the Confidential Information, including but not limited to, sections 2,4,5,6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and/or return of all Confidential Information from the latter date of either termination or return of such information. Neither this Agreement nor any rights hereunder, in whole or in part, shall be assignable or otherwise transferable by either Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Term, Assignment and Survival: This Agreement shall automatically renew every year unless one party provides 30-day written notice of termination. Each Party’s obligations with respect to the Confidential Information shall automatically terminate upon the termination of this Agreement. This Agreement and all rights hereunder, in whole or in part, shall be freely assignable or otherwise transferable by either Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement can only be terminated by mutual written agreement and that obligations survive termination. This also states that the agreement is not assignable. The modified version creates an automatic renewal process that happens every year and states that obligations don't survive termination and now it is assignable. This contradicts the original conditions and creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration, obligations, and transferability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Protection of "Confidential Information": In consideration of each Party's disclosure of Confidential Information to the other Party, each Party agrees with respect to the Confidential Information received from the other Party, that it: (a) shall maintain such Confidential Information in the strictest confidence; Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096 www.Pareteum.com NYSE MKT: TEUM (b) shall not disclose, transfer or otherwise make available any of such Confidential Information to any third party, unless such Confidential Information must be disclosed for the purposes contemplated herein, or under legal compulsion to disclose any such Confidential Information, in which event each Party shall, prior to such disclosure, obtain written consent from the other Party and obtain from the third person a written agreement acknowledging the binding effect of these restrictions regarding the Confidential Information; and (c) shall not directly, indirectly or in concert with any person, use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than evaluating the prospective business relationship with the other Party in accordance with the introduction . Each Party shall take reasonable measures to protect the Confidential Information of the other Party. Those measures shall not be less than the measures taken to protect the receiving Party’s own confidential information. Confidential Information of the other Party may be provided to a Party’s employees only on a need-to-know basis, and prior to such provision, the Party will notify each employee to whom such disclosure is made that such Confidential Information is received in confidence and shall be kept in confidence by such employee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 directly contradicts the contradicted text in Section 2. Section 9 originally stated that obligations survive termination and the agreement is not assignable, while the modified Section 9 states that obligations automatically terminate upon termination and the agreement is freely assignable. Section 2 details specific obligations regarding Confidential Information, including maintaining its strictest confidence and not disclosing it to third parties. The change in Section 9, stating that these obligations terminate upon agreement termination, directly contradicts the purpose and enforceability of Section 2.
